description: Date-anchored deterministic historical replay fixtures for the Economic Intelligence Layer. No live data. No API calls. All driver states are manually set based on known historical conditions and approximated using the Sprint 4A/B driver state vocabulary.
engine_limitations: inflation driver is always unavailable in Sprint 4A/B — 2022 inflation shocks cannot be modeled via driver state; approximated through credit/risk_appetite/interest_rates signals only.
liquidity driver is always unavailable in Sprint 4A/B — tightening/easing direction cannot be expressed; approximated through interest_rates/credit signals.
usd, valuation, consumer_behaviour, growth drivers are unavailable — limits scenario accuracy.
Driver state vocabulary has no directionality (active/watch only) — 'easing rates' and 'rising rates' both map to active_shadow_inferred; context must be recorded in scenario notes.

Theme taxonomy covers: data_centre_power, semiconductors, banks, energy, defence, quality_cash_flow, defensive_quality, small_caps. high_multiple_growth, gold_safe_haven, and value/dividend themes are not in current taxonomy.

scenarios.description: US CPI peaked at 9.1% (June 2022). Fed hiking at fastest pace in 40 years. Yields spike, risk-off conditions, growth/high-multiple selloff. Limitation: inflation driver unavailable in Sprint 4A/B — approximated via interest_rates + credit + risk_appetite signals.
expected_blocked_or_conditional_rules: banks_rates_rising: conditional on yield curve direction — not automatic structural buy
inflation driver unavailable: 2022 rate shock partially expressed only
engine_limitations: inflation driver unavailable — rate shock approximated through interest_rates + credit
high_multiple_growth theme not in taxonomy — growth selloff not directly testable

scenarios.description: Russia invades Ukraine (Feb 24, 2022). Oil spikes toward $130. Defence budgets surge across NATO. Commodity shock. Energy and defence themes expected to activate/watchlist.
expected_blocked_or_conditional_rules: gold_safe_haven: not in current taxonomy — unavailable
inflation driver unavailable: commodity inflation pressures partially expressed only
engine_limitations: inflation driver unavailable — commodity shock partially expressed through oil_energy
gold_safe_haven theme not in taxonomy — safe haven demand not testable
airline/transport headwind not in taxonomy — consumer impact not testable

scenarios.description: ChatGPT / GPT-4 launches trigger AI capex cycle (2023 Q1–Q2). NVDA +120% YTD by May 2023. Data centre, semiconductor, power demand themes emerge. AI capex growth is the dominant structural tailwind. This is the scenario the Sprint 4A/B engine is best designed to express.
engine_limitations: Current engine is well-suited for this scenario — ai_infrastructure_tailwind_shadow is the primary designed regime.

scenarios.description: US 10-year yield hits 5.0% (Oct 2023). Small caps, high-multiple growth under pressure. Banks conditional — yield curve still inverted. Fed peak rate uncertainty. Risk posture cautious/selective.
expected_blocked_or_conditional_rules: banks_rates_rising: conditional — inverted yield curve limits NIM benefit; watchlist only, not automatic structural buy
high_multiple_growth headwind: not in taxonomy — growth pressure not directly modeled
engine_limitations: Yield curve inversion (inverted NIM) not expressible in current driver state vocabulary
high_multiple_growth theme not in taxonomy
growth driver unavailable — growth pressure not directly testable

scenarios.description: Fed signals/begins cutting rates (Sep 2024 first cut). Selective risk-on. AI capex cycle ongoing. Credit broadly contained. Liquidity improving. Limitation: driver state vocab has no 'easing' direction — 'watch' used as best approximation for transitioning/declining rates. Regime may appear cautious rather than risk-on due to vocabulary constraint.
expected_blocked_or_conditional_rules: rate_cut_direction: not expressible in current driver state vocabulary — both rising and easing rates map to watch_shadow_inferred
selective_risk_on: current posture logic returns cautious when credit=watch + risk_appetite=watch + no active driver; risk-on nuance not expressible
engine_limitations: Driver state vocabulary has no directionality (active/watch only) — 'easing rates' cannot be distinguished from 'still-high rates at watch'
liquidity driver unavailable — improving liquidity conditions not expressible
Regime defaulting to credit_stress_watch_shadow is a known Sprint 4A/B limitation for watch-only scenarios; Sprint 5B fixture documents this gap for Sprint 6 resolution

scenarios.description: COVID-19 global lockdowns (March 2020). VIX >80. Credit markets seize. Fed emergency QE. Risk-off extreme. Modeled as shock phase only — policy support/recovery phase not modeled (liquidity driver unavailable). Defensive and quality themes expected to activate.
expected_blocked_or_conditional_rules: liquidity stress/support not expressible — Fed QE impact not modeled
recovery_phase: not modeled — only shock phase included
engine_limitations: liquidity driver unavailable — QE / policy support not expressible
VIX >80 extreme case — volatility active_shadow_inferred is the best approximation
Recovery/rebound phase (April 2020+) not modeled in this fixture
